summary: Iran's negotiating team insists on a permanent ceasefire and full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon as key conditions for any final agreement, with attacks on Lebanon decreasing notably over the past six days. The IDF targeted Hezbollah's 'southern front,' including strikes on Azeiza and the neutralization of Nasser's commanders, while Netanyahu stated the IDF controls nearly 70% of the Gaza Strip. Iranian hacking group 'Handala' commander was reportedly killed during 'Operation Lion,' and Iranian Petroleum Minister called for the withdrawal of all US military forces from West Asia at BRICS.
key_points: Iran's negotiating team stated that full Israeli withdrawal from occupied Lebanese territories is a key condition and a 'red line' for any final agreement, with attacks on Lebanon decreasing notably over the past six days. (Source: @SharghDaily/168071)
IDF activity targeted Hezbollah's 'southern front,' including the Azeiza strike, neutralization of Nasser's commanders, and seizure of Bader's asset. (Source: @idf_telegram/24444)
Iranian hacking group 'Handala' commander Yia Hossein Fangaki was killed during 'Operation Lion,' according to reports from Iran. (Source: @inon_yttach/9157)
Iranian Petroleum Minister Mohsen Paknejad called at BRICS for the withdrawal of all US military forces from West Asia and dismantling of American bases in the region. (Source: @presstv/195618)
Netanyahu stated the IDF controls nearly 70% of the Gaza Strip and that there are further missions ahead against Iran, Hezbollah, and Hamas. (Source: @New_security8200/202084)
Iranian sources stated Iran will not accept less than a permanent ceasefire and complete Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372806)

US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Gulf Arab states do not support Iran collecting tolls for Strait of Hormuz transit, and no country supports paying for strait passage. (Source: @VahidOnline/76663)
IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir emphasized the military needs every combat soldier and commander, amid controversy over integrating women into the Armored Corps. (Source: @manniefabian/58681)

sections.executive_summary: Iran's negotiating team insists on a permanent ceasefire and full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon as key conditions for any final agreement, with attacks on Lebanon decreasing notably over the past six days. The IDF targeted Hezbollah's 'southern front,' including strikes on Azeiza and the neutralization of Nasser's commanders, while Netanyahu stated the IDF controls nearly 70% of the Gaza Strip. Iranian hacking group 'Handala' commander was reportedly killed during 'Operation Lion,' and Iranian Petroleum Minister called for the withdrawal of all US military forces from West Asia at BRICS.

points: Iran's negotiating team stated that full Israeli withdrawal from occupied Lebanese territories is a key condition and a 'red line' for any final agreement. A noted decrease in attacks on Lebanon over the past six days suggests Iranian influence may be effective, but Iran will not be satisfied with anything less than permanent ceasefire and complete withdrawal. (Source: @SharghDaily/168071)

Iranian Petroleum Minister Mohsen Paknejad called at BRICS for the withdrawal of all US military forces from West Asia and dismantling of American bases in the region, calling it the only path to global energy stability. (Source: @presstv/195618)

Iranian hacking group 'Handala,' operating under Iran's Intelligence Ministry, claimed its commander Yia Hossein Fangaki was killed during 'Operation Lion.' The group claimed responsibility for hacking accounts of former PM Netanyahu and former Chief of Staff Herzi Halevi, as well as sending psychological terror messages to thousands of Israelis. (Source: @inon_yttach/9157)
Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad-Baqer Kalibaf denied Trump's claims that Iran agreed to purchase agricultural products from the US using unfrozen asset funds. (Source: @asafroz/50732)
Iranian sources stated Iran will not accept less than a permanent ceasefire and complete Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372806)
Iran is reportedly negotiating with Oman for a supervisory mechanism and collection of tolls for transit through the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @VahidOnline/76663)

IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir emphasized the military needs 'every male and female combat soldier and every male and female commander,' amid controversy over a pilot program integrating women into the Armored Corps and threats by rabbis to stop sending students to tank units. (Source: @manniefabian/58681)
IDF activity targeted Hezbollah's 'southern front,' including the Azeiza strike, neutralization of Nasser's commanders, and seizure of Bader's asset. (Source: @idf_telegram/24444)

Netanyahu stated the IDF controls nearly 70% of the Gaza Strip and that there are further missions ahead against Iran, Hezbollah, and Hamas. He also stated Israel will remain in the Lebanese security belt as long as required and that Iran will not obtain a nuclear weapon. (Source: @New_security8200/202084)

An Israeli Arab terrorist suspect from Umm al-Fahm was arrested by police and Shin Bet on suspicion of serious security activity; two M16 rifles, a Carlo-type weapon, and magazines were found in his home. (Source: @inon_yttach/9166)

The helicopter carrying Israeli President Isaac Herzog was forced to make an emergency landing at Palmachim Air Base after a mid-flight bird strike; no injuries were reported. (Source: @PressTV/195627)
Three people were killed in an attack in southern Lebanon. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93224)
Netanyahu stated the IDF controls nearly 70% of the Gaza Strip and has returned security and prosperity to the Gaza envelope. (Source: @New_security8200/202084)
Reports indicate one person was killed and several injured in a structure strike in Gaza City. (Source: @yediotnews25/131928)
An Israeli Arab terrorist suspect from Umm al-Fahm in the West Bank was arrested on suspicion of serious security activity, with weapons found in his home. (Source: @inon_yttach/9166)

Iran's negotiating team stated that full Israeli withdrawal from occupied Lebanese territories is a key condition and a 'red line' for any final agreement. A noted decrease in attacks on Lebanon over the past six days suggests Iranian influence may be effective. (Source: @SharghDaily/168071)
Three people were killed in an attack in southern Lebanon. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93224)
Hostile aerial activity continued over Lebanon, with public warnings issued to exercise caution. (Source: @redlinkleb/28202)

Italy and France announced a new coalition to support Lebanon once the current UNIFIL mandate expires at year's end, following talks in Antibes between PM Meloni and President Macron. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4336)
Netanyahu stated Israel will remain in the Lebanese security belt as long as required and that Israel will not withdraw. (Source: @New_security8200/202084)

Iraq is considering leaving OPEC due to oil production quotas, following a severe financial crisis related to export disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. Iraq warned that if oil exporter groups do not significantly increase its production quota, it may withdraw from OPEC. (Source: @SharghDaily/168078)

US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Gulf Arab states do not support Iran collecting tolls for Strait of Hormoz transit, and no country supports paying for strait passage. Oman also emphasized that no transit tolls will be applied in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @VahidOnline/76663)
There is agreement among Gulf states that Iran must not collect payments in the Strait of Hormuz, whether called fees or tariffs. (Source: @asafroz/50730)

The IRGC attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship near the coast of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz, causing damage but no injuries. This attack puts the agreement to the test. (Source: @N12chat/214671)

The International Maritime Organization temporarily suspended the evacuation of stranded ships and sailors from Hormuz following an attack on a ship in the Gulf of Oman. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372811)
57 ships with approximately 1,100 sailors and crew sailed through the Strait of Hormuz in the last two days, according to UN data published by Reuters. (Source: @asafroz/50723)

key_developments: Iran's negotiating team insists on full Israeli withdrawal from occupied Lebanese territories as a 'red line' condition for any final agreement, with attacks on Lebanon decreasing notably over the past six days. (Source: @SharghDaily/168071)
IDF targeted Hezbollah's 'southern front' with strikes on Azeiza, neutralization of Nasser's commanders, and seizure of Bader's asset. (Source: @idf_telegram/24444)
Iranian hacking group 'Handala' commander Yia Hossein Fangaki was killed during 'Operation Lion,' according to reports from Iran. (Source: @inon_yttach/9157)
Iranian Petroleum Minister called at BRICS for withdrawal of all US military forces from West Asia and dismantling of American bases. (Source: @presstv/195618)

Netanyahu stated IDF controls nearly 70% of Gaza Strip and further missions remain against Iran, Hezbollah, and Hamas; Israel will not withdraw from Lebanese security belt. (Source: @New_security8200/202084)
IRGC attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship near Oman near Strait of Hormuz, causing damage but no injuries, putting the agreement to the test. (Source: @N12chat/214671)
Iraq is considering leaving OPEC due to oil production quotas, following a severe financial crisis related to export disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @SharghDaily/168078)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment remains volatile across multiple fronts. Iran's insistence on full Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon as a non-negotiable 'red line' suggests diplomatic friction will continue, while the decrease in attacks on Lebanon over six days may indicate temporary de-escalation or Iranian pressure on Hezbollah. The IDF's continued operations against Hezbollah's southern front and the assertion of control over nearly 70% of Gaza signal sustained Israeli offensive pressure. The IRGC's attack on a Singapore-flagged vessel near the Strait of Hormuz introduces a maritime threat dimension that could jeopardize emerging agreements on Gulf shipping tolls. The killing of the Handala hacking group commander represents a potential escalation in the cyber domain.
sections.regional_response: Gulf Arab states, through US Secretary Rubio's statements, have aligned against Iran's attempts to collect transit tolls in the Strait of Hormuz, with Oman explicitly stating no tolls will be applied. Italy and France are positioning for a post-UNIFIL Lebanon support coalition, signaling international recognition of the need for alternative security arrangements when the current mandate expires at year's end. Iraq's potential OPEC exit over production quota disputes could further destabilize energy markets already affected by Hormuz export disruptions. Iran's BRICS platform is being used to push for complete US military withdrawal from West Asia, linking energy security to geopolitical demands.
sections.intelligence_notes: The reported decrease in attacks on Lebanon over six days, coupled with Iran's public insistence on withdrawal conditions, suggests Tehran may be exercising command influence over Hezbollah operations as a negotiating lever. The Handala group's claimed cyber operations against Israeli leadership accounts indicate Iranian-linked hacking remains an active intelligence collection and psychological operations tool. The IRGC's maritime attack near Hormuz, occurring alongside negotiations over strait tolls, may represent internal factional signaling or a deliberate pressure tactic. Netanyahu's public claim of 70% control of Gaza, combined with stated missions against Iran, Hezbollah, and Hamas, indicates the conflict is far from concluded and multi-front operations will continue.
